title: Peter Pan : $b [Peter and Wendy]
author: Barrie, J. M. (James Matthew)
summary: Peter Pan begins in the Darling household where Mrs. and Mr. Darling, with children Wendy, John, and Michael, keep an orderly nursery tended by Nana, a prim Newfoundland nurse-dog, and maid Liza. Wendy discovers a recurring name, Peter, sketched repeatedly across her mind; she believes Peter Pan visits at night, leaving unfamiliar skeleton leaves by the high nursery window. Mr. Darling dismisses such talk as nonsense, but Mrs. Darling is perplexed. The chapter describes the parents’ domestic routines, financial fussing, children’s imagined Neverlands, and the cozy, teasing tone that makes Peter’s impending intrusion feel both real and whimsical.
